Completion is not accepted from prose alone. End with a structured acceptance report.

Finish with a fenced JSON block tagged `acceptance-report` in this shape:
Use empty arrays when no items apply; array fields contain strings unless object entries are shown.
`criteriaSatisfied[].status` must be exactly one of: satisfied, not-satisfied, not-applicable.
`commandsRun[].result` must be exactly one of: passed, failed, not-run.
`manualNotes` and `notes` are optional strings; an empty string means no note and does not satisfy `manual-notes` evidence.

validationOutput: Q1 (scan 7 locations): PASS — DEFAULT_LOCATIONS dict has all 7 entries
Q2 (SHA256 matches import_model.py): PASS — 65536-byte chunks, hashlib.sha256(), .hexdigest()

Q5 (--execute calls model-shelf import correctly): PASS — builds correct CLI args with --org/--repo

residualRisks: I/O-bound SHA256 scan on TB-scale machines — no checkpoint/restart mechanism
Script depends on `model-shelf` CLI on $PATH — no pre-flight validation

childReport.diffSummary: New scripts/model-shelf-migrate (340 lines, stdlib-only) + tests/test_migrate.py (12 tests). No existing files modified.
reviewFindings: high: scripts/model-shelf-migrate:138-157 — infer_org_repo uses parent.name as repo for file-like paths; import_model.py:_infer_org_repo_gguf:184 uses source.stem. Heuristics are inconsistent though mitigated by explicit --org/--repo passthrough.
medium: scripts/model-shelf-migrate:120-136 — detect_quant_from_filename is defined and tested but never called in main(); dead code.
medium: scripts/model-shelf-migrate:48 — MODEL_EXTENSIONS includes .bin/.pt/.pth/.onnx, none supported by import_model for single-file import. These files hash but fail on --execute.
low: scripts/model-shelf-migrate:366 — Non-.gguf single files fall through to fmt='gguf' default; import_model subprocess rejects them.
low: scripts/model-shelf-migrate:157 — Missing hyphen heuristic on grandparent org inference (import_model checks '-' in grandparent; migration does not).
note: scripts/model-shelf-migrate:93-94 — Non-existent-path handling in format detection added for test ergonomics; existing-path logic matches import_model.
note: scripts/model-shelf-migrate:222 — _is_model_like allows extensionless blobs; import_model can't auto-detect format for these.
childReport.manualNotes: The script is functionally correct for its primary use case (GGUF migration from scattered locations). The org/repo inference difference is a deliberate design choice that yields better repo names for the migration workflow, but diverges from import_model's heuristic. Dead quant code and unsupported extensions in MODEL_EXTENSIONS are polish issues, not blockers. The non-GGUF single-file import gap is a real limitation if users have stray .safetensors or .bin files outside proper directories.
